revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Shinto Shrine | name = Hayashi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:林神社(砺波市頼成）.JPG|270px|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami City, Yorase)]]
Entrance to the precinct | location = {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|ja|富山県}}, {{ill|Tonami City|ja|砺波市}}, Yorase 1720 | ISO = JP-16 | coordinates = {{Coord|36|38|27.067|N|137|1|5.83|E|region:JP-16_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Toyama | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Hi no Omi no Mikoto|ja|日臣命}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Bunmei 7 (1376) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} | alternate_name = | festival = | Rituals = }} '''Hayashi Shrine''' (林神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Yorase, Tonami City, Toyama Prefecture. It is identified as a possible {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho}}, though there is debate with another shrine in Hayashi, Tonami City, also bearing the same name. Currently, both shrines are considered "disputable sites" (論社) for the title. == References == * Tonami City History Compilation Committee, *Tonami City History* (pp. 126–137), Tonami City, 1965. {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Hayashi Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|林神社 (砺波市頼成)|version=75991021|comment=Imported full ja history}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Hayashi Shrine (Tonami) (Q22120029)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Etchū Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | RONSHA || {{ill|Hayashi Shrine (Tonami)|lt=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami)|ja|林神社 (砺波市頼成)|qid=Q22120029|1=Hayashi Shrine (Tonami)}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || — || — || {{ill|Hayashino Shrine|lt=Hayashino Shrine|qid=Q135040352|1=Hayashino Shrine}} || {{coord|36.640852|137.018286|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=182388 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q22120029}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| | name = Hayashi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:林神社(砺波市頼成）.JPG | location = {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tonami, Toyama|en|Tonami, Toyama|qid=Q526760|lt=Tonami City|1=Tonami, Toyama}}, Yorase 1720 | ISO = JP-16 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.640852|N|137.018286|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Toyama | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Hi no Omi no Mikoto|ja|日臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Omi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道臣命|1=Michinoomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641798}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Bunmei 7 (1376) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_name = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Tonami|qid=Q526760|1=Tonami, Toyama}} }} '''Hayashi Shrine''' (林神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Yorase, Tonami City, Toyama Prefecture. It is identified as a possible {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though there is debate with another shrine in Hayashi, Tonami City, also bearing the same name. Currently, both shrines are considered "disputable sites" (論社) for the title. == References == * Tonami City History Compilation Committee, *Tonami City History* (pp. 126–137), Tonami City, 1965. {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|林神社 (砺波市頼成)|version=75991021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q22120029}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Infobox religious building| | name = Hayashi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:林神社(砺波市頼成）.JPG | location = {{ill|Toyama Prefecture|en|Toyama Prefecture|qid=Q132929|lt=Toyama Prefecture|1=Toyama Prefecture}}, {{ill|Tonami, Toyama|en|Tonami, Toyama|qid=Q526760|lt=Tonami City|1=Tonami, Toyama}}, Yorase 1720 | ISO = JP-16 | coordinates = {{Coord|36.640852|N|137.018286|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Map = Japan Toyama | Enshrined Deities = {{ill|Hi no Omi no Mikoto|ja|日臣命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hi no Omi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 道臣命|1=Michinoomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641798}}, {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Toyouke Omikami|ja|豊受大神|lt=Toyouke Ōmikami|1=Toyouke-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke Omikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨウケビメ|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2983724}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|qid=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku|1=Emperor Sutoku}} | type = Former Village Shrine | consecration_year = Bunmei 7 (1376) | Main Hall Style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=Shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | alternate_name = | festival = | Rituals = | location = {{ill|Tonami|qid=Q526760|1=Tonami, Toyama}} }} '''Hayashi Shrine''' (林神社) is a Shinto shrine located in Yorase, Tonami City, Toyama Prefecture. It is identified as a possible {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, though there is debate with another shrine in Hayashi, Tonami City, also bearing the same name. Currently, both shrines are considered "disputable sites" (論社) for the title. == References == * Tonami City History Compilation Committee, *Tonami City History* (pp. 126–137), Tonami City, 1965. {{Shinto-stub}} {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{translated page|ja|林神社 (砺波市頼成)|version=75991021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q22120029}} ==Notes== [[da:d:Q22120029]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tonami]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Tonami, Toyama]] [[Category:Hayashi Shrine (Tonami)]] [[Category:History of Tonami, Toyama]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Etchū Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha_in_Etchū_Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Toyama Prefecture]] [[Category:Tonami history]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
